job_title: Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology
company: New York City Department of Housing Preservation & Development
strengths: Python ETL and data pipeline experience
weaknesses: Limited explicit Microsoft Access / SQL Developer mention
No stated experience with HPD or housing programs
Community coordination / community work experience not shown
Limited tenure in data roles (≈2+ years)
improvement_bullets: Developed and maintained complex BI dashboards using Power BI and Oracle BI concepts, providing technical training and documentation to cross-functional teams to foster a data-driven culture.
Led stakeholder collaboration initiatives to define business requirements, effectively translating policy development needs into actionable, performance-based indicators and forecasting models.
Managed database workflows using SQL Developer and Microsoft Access, ensuring data integrity while supporting administrative reporting and system-wide data governance.

Streamlined operational outcomes for community-focused services by analyzing demographic and geographic datasets, directly supporting the mission of enhancing housing preservation through data-backed strategies.
Served as a subject matter expert on analytics tools, coaching agency staff on advanced reporting and dashboard functionalities to improve organizational capacity and user self-sufficiency.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology opportunity with the New York City Department of Housing Preservation & Development. I am interested in this opportunity because of HPD’s commitment to utilizing data-driven insights to foster safe, affordable housing and neighborhood strength for all New Yorkers. What interests me about working for HPD is its proactive approach to addressing building distress and improving neighborhood conditions, which inspires me as a data professional dedicated to public service. I believe my expertise in BI dashboard development and my background in complex data modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating raw, disparate data into intuitive visual narratives that drive operational efficiency. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I replaced manual reporting with interactive Power BI dashboards, which improved KPI visibility and reduced data processing time by 40%. Ultimately, I know that the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology role at HPD would allow me to bring my technical proficiency in SQL and Python to the task of enhancing ENS operations. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my stakeholder collaboration skills. I led the documentation of SOPs for operational workflows, which reduced new hire onboarding time by 50% and ensured consistent service delivery across the team. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in data storytelling in working on the development of preservation planning and forecasting at HPD. I know that my technical versatility and commitment to data-driven decision-making align with what it takes to succeed at the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
